zh.lifeSummary: Joan Barry是Richard Barry的女兒，出生在英國。她與William Redham結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Barry是Richard Barry的女儿，出生在英国。她与William Redham结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Barr
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow) and northern Irish (Ulster): habitational name from any of various places in southwestern Scotland, in particular Ayrshire and Renfrewshire, named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’ or a Brittonic cognate of this.

English and Welsh: habitational name from Great Barr in Staffordshire. This is from Brittonic barro- ‘top, summit’, referring to Barr Beacon; there is evidence that this was known as la Bare in the 13th century.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Barre-en-Ouche in Eure, France, or perhaps from Barre-de-Semilly in Manche, France.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Scottish (Angus): habitational name from Barry in Angus or from another place so named, generally named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’, or from a British cognate of this word. This name is also the Scottish form of Barry .
French (Barrié): variant of Barrier .
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

variants.name: Berry
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Dubarry
meanings: French: variant of the topographic or habitational name Barry , with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’.

variants.name: Baril
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Alain, Armand, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean-Marc, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Monique, Phillippe.
French: from Old French baril ‘barrel’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cooper, or a nickname for a rotund man. Compare Barry 7 and Berry 7.

History: Jean Baril from the Saintonge region of France was in QC by 1673, when he married Marie Guillet, the first of his three wives. He died in 1724 in Batiscan, QC. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Barrie, Bar(r)y, and Berry.

variants.name: Bary
meanings: Walloon and northern French: variant of   Barry   8.
French (southern): rare variant of   Barry   6.
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Barry was born in England as the daughter of Richard Barry. She married William Redham. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Barry est née à Angleterre, fille de Richard Barry. Elle a épousé William Redham. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Barry wurde in England, als Tochter von Richard Barry geboren. Sie hat William Redham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Barry è nata il nel England come figlia di Richard Barry. Lei sposato William Redham. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan BarryはイギリスでRichard Barryの娘として生まれた。William Redhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Barry(은)는 영국에서 Richard Barry의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 William Redham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Barry nasceu em Inglaterra filha de Richard Barry. Ela casou-se com William Redham. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Barry родилась в местности Англия. Её отца звали Richard Barry. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Redham. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Joan Barry nació en Inglaterra como hija de Richard Barry. Se casó con William Redham. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
